title: age-group — HTTP middleware
description: The age-group module ships ZERO middleware of its own. Age groups are reference data, not a request-scoped scoping axis — there is no `X-Age-Group-Id` header, no active-age-group resolution, no per-request context. Every endpoint reads the age_group from a route parameter or from the request body; there is no cross-cutting middleware need. Contrast with region + branch which both ship active-resolver middleware because the caller's active region + branch legitimately vary per request.
notes: Downstream modules that scope by age_group (Team, EventDivision, TeamRegistration) filter their queries by age_group_id from the request body or route param — they don't consume an active-age-group context.

The AgeGroupResolver service accepts (dob, sport_key, on_date) as explicit arguments — never inferred from request state. This is deliberate: caller intent must be explicit for every eligibility resolution, especially given the compliance-critical downstream Athlete impact.
